tool_input.brief:
BRIEF (fork fork_moj8vtgo_e960c2, context_mode=brief):

Goal: Wrap the Chambers platform in a Capacitor shell so it ships as an iOS+Android app while continuing to live as a Vercel-deployed Next.js app.

**Architecture invariant:** Chambers is a multi-tenant platform. Every request resolves a tenant_id from hostname + JWT custom claim and is RLS-scoped on tenant_id. The Capacitor shell does NOT bypass this. Tenant resolution happens server-side per-request as before; the Capacitor wrapper is purely a runtime container. Do not introduce a single-tenant collapse on the mobile build.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Capacitor build produces signed .apk + .ipa using the existing Vercel-served frontend as the webview.
2. After every git push to the chambers-frontend repo, poll Vercel until the deployment for that commit reports READY (not just QUEUED/BUILDING). Do not declare done until READY.
3. Multi-tenant flows (login on tenant A vs tenant B subdomain) work in the wrapped shell.
4. RLS policies still enforced on every API request from the wrapped client.

Doctrine pointers:
- ~/ecodiaos/patterns/deploy-verify-or-the-fork-didnt-finish.md
- ~/ecodiaos/patterns/brief-names-the-product-not-the-immediate-task.md
- ~/ecodiaos/clients/chambers.md

Branch: fix/capacitor-wrap-2026-04-29 from main on chambers-frontend.
